summary: OpenTelemetry tracing should rely on low-cardinality attributes, manual spans around critical stages, and dashboards that correlate latency with backend pressure.
Redis cache quality depends on hit rate, evictions, slowlog behavior, fragmentation, and whether hotkeys or bigkeys are increasing.
Search quality should be observed through latency, result count, freshness satisfaction, authority hit rate, and sampled review signals instead of one aggregate score.
A good scheduler republishes pending jobs, expires stale searches, cleans summary cache, and inspects Redis for bigkeys and hotkeys on a fixed cadence.
Online AI review should score groundedness, faithfulness, completeness, and send low-quality samples into a bad-case stream for follow-up.
Import quality needs per-document-type metrics so PDF, scanned PDF, Word, HTML, markdown, and plain text regressions can be compared directly.
